name: B2B Partnerships Promo
summary: A row in a spreadsheet comes out as a finished, personalized set of assets for a partner — flyer, messaging image and email — without anyone opening a design file.
objectives: One personalized set of assets per partner, per campaign — without anyone rebuilding a file by hand.
One partner list that campaigns are built from, instead of re-typing people into each one.
Partners keep their own details up to date, themselves.
Every market in its own language.

outcomes.text: A row in a spreadsheet comes out the other end as a finished flyer, a messaging image and an email — co-branded, personalized, and filed in the right folder for its region, campaign and partner. Real campaigns ship out of this.
Partners register and update themselves through a form, and that list is what campaigns are built from. Setting up a campaign no longer means re-typing the people in it.
The form is built for what actually goes wrong: two people editing at once, bad data typed in, and text that tries to behave like code. All three are handled.
Nothing goes out by hand. The email passes a check that can stop it, and the spreadsheet's own code is deployed from version control instead of pasted into a window.
It runs on real campaigns. Every fix since it launched came from watching people use it, not from a plan written beforehand.

An independent review of the whole pipeline became shipped safeguards, not a document: tests block a build-breaking change before production, the button refuses to run from a partner-list tab, untrusted text can't inject markup, and every email is checked as it's made.

milestones.title: Agree one spreadsheet layout, and one set of placeholders for every asset
Make the partner list the thing campaigns are built from
Ship the form partners use to register themselves
Handle what real use breaks: two editors at once, bad input, lost rows
Deploy the spreadsheet's code from version control instead of by hand
One email pipeline, checked before anything goes out
Run it on real campaigns and fix what that exposed
Turn an independent review of the whole pipeline into shipped safeguards
Decide if the form needs its own Spanish, Portuguese and Turkish versions, now that the team uses it
